pages.purpose: Homepage explaining Demeo and entry points to the demo room and dashboard.

important_elements.label: Try the Demo Room
important_elements.why: Primary entry into the prospect-facing demo room.

pages.purpose: Founder setup page for company and product context.

important_elements.label: One-liner description
important_elements.selector_hint: textarea[placeholder="We help B2B SaaS teams turn cold outreach into qualified pipeline with AI demo rooms."]

important_elements.label: Target audience field
important_elements.selector_hint: input[placeholder="Solo founders, lean B2B sales teams, Series A startups"]

allowed_actions.label: Highlight setup form
allowed_actions.intent: Show what the founder provides during setup

pages.purpose: Agent lab page showing available specialist agent pages.
Operator dashboard for turning product context into a GTM flow.

important_elements.label: Product prompt area
important_elements.selector_hint: textarea:has-text("We're a developer-first analytics platform that helps engineering teams understand product usage in real-time, with zero instrumentation.")
important_elements.why: Shows the founder input used by the orchestrator.

important_elements.label: Master orchestrator pipeline
important_elements.why: Illustrates agent stages across the GTM system.

allowed_actions.label: Highlight founder prompt
allowed_actions.intent: Show what the founder provides to the operator

allowed_actions.label: Generate GTM Flow
allowed_actions.intent: Generate the GTM flow from provided context

pages.purpose: Prospect-facing AI demo room for a named buyer persona.

important_elements.label: Starter question
important_elements.selector_hint: button:has-text("Show me how this works for my team")
important_elements.why: Safe preset question to guide the demo.

allowed_actions.label: Highlight personalized demo context
allowed_actions.intent: Show the prospect gets a tailored room

allowed_actions.label: End and view CRM
allowed_actions.intent: Show what happens after the demo completes

pages.purpose: CRM summary showing lead score, objections, and generated follow-up.

allowed_actions.label: Highlight generated follow-up
allowed_actions.intent: Show the founder what they receive after the demo

pages.purpose: About page with founder names and company mission.

flows.goal: Show the end-to-end founder journey from setup to post-demo follow-up.

steps.say: First, the founder provides company and product context here.
Then the operator turns that context into a GTM flow.
The prospect gets a personalized demo room.
After the demo, the founder sees lead scoring and the generated follow-up.
Content generation lives in its own agent page.
Legal review is separate and source-grounded.
The Demo Agent opens the prospect-facing room.

flows.goal: Show the buyer-facing demo room and CRM handoff.

steps.say: This room is personalized for the buyer.
When the demo ends, the CRM summary appears.

approved_answers.topic: What does the founder need to provide?
approved_answers.answer: A product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

approved_answers.topic: Should the runtime click the real app directly?
approved_answers.answer: The MVP should use an approved manifest and visual event playback. Playwright is better for setup-time extraction and validation. Live app control can come later for approved low-risk paths.
Yes. Gemini Live or a LiveKit voice agent can call the same safe demo tools used by text: show page, move cursor, highlight, propose click, answer from knowledge, and update lead memory.

notes: Avoided inventing selectors not present in crawled data.
Omitted unsafe submit/send actions; only navigation, highlighting, and clearly safe generation clicks are included.

decisions.question: What does the founder need to provide?
decisions.reply: A product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

events.say: Here’s the founder setup page.
This form captures the company and product context.
A product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

decisions.reason: Direct approved answer plus the setup page that shows what the founder provides.

decisions.question: Show me the agent/content console.
decisions.reply: The agent lab is the approved entry point here; the content console itself is not separately exposed in the manifest.

events.say: This is the agent lab.
The agent lab is the approved entry point here; the content console itself is not separately exposed in the manifest.

decisions.reason: No safe manifest path exists to a standalone content page, so I can only show the agent lab and explain the limitation.

decisions.question: Show the prospect demo room.
decisions.reply: This is the buyer-facing demo room, personalized for the prospect.

events.say: Here’s the prospect demo room.
This room is personalized for the buyer.

decisions.reason: Approved flow includes showing the demo room and highlighting its personalized context.

decisions.question: Show me what the founder receives after the demo.
decisions.reply: After the demo, the founder gets lead scoring, objections handled, and a generated follow-up email.

events.say: Here’s the CRM summary after the demo.
This is the generated follow-up the founder receives.

decisions.reason: Approved CRM page shows the post-demo output and follow-up.

success.success_rule: MVP-usable means at least 3/4 expected app areas discovered, at least 6 safe/reviewable actions, no invented manifest pages/action refs, and at least 3/4 prospect questions planned to the right page/action.
